required_inputs: request intent and intended operational action
healthcare operations boundary, decision-rights matrix, and adjacent specialist map
clinical, privacy, payer, licensure, and executive authority indicators
refusal_triggers: request asks for diagnosis, treatment, clinical protocol change, patient-specific clinical priority, or staffing licensure decision
request asks for payer coverage, authorization approval, reimbursement interpretation, or PHI disclosure outside tenant controls

required_inputs: public healthcare regulator, payer/provider, workflow, and measure sources
tenant operating policies, source hierarchy, and source freshness metadata
current facility, service-line, and jurisdiction context
refusal_triggers: required public or tenant authority source is missing or stale
public guidance, tenant policy, payer rule, facility procedure, or jurisdictional requirement materially conflicts

required_inputs: daily huddle notes, throughput tracker, staffing roster, bed or capacity board, scheduling record, downtime note, ITSM ticket, and escalation log
patient/member, service date, authorization, referral, eligibility, privacy, and system-of-record context when operationally required
audit and completion evidence requirements
refusal_triggers: critical operational artifact is absent, stale, contradictory, or not retrievable
traceability from blocker to owner to completion evidence cannot be established

required_inputs: systems-of-record map across EHR, ADT, scheduling, bed/capacity, staffing, ITSM, authorization/referral/eligibility, privacy, and audit systems
tenant RACI, routing matrix, service-line checklist, escalation SLA, and prerequisite rules
capacity, staffing, access, scheduled-work, downtime, referral, authorization, and eligibility dependencies
refusal_triggers: critical dependency owner is unresolved
systems of record materially disagree on patient location, schedule, capacity status, staffing coverage, authorization, referral, eligibility, owner, or closure state

required_inputs: evidence completeness signals
authority completeness signals
policy conflict indicators
patient-safety, privacy, payer, staffing, and system-risk indicators
refusal_triggers: missing tenant facts or authority maps would change operational disposition
clinical risk, PHI handling, payer interpretation, staffing licensure, owner conflict, or source conflict prevents safe synthesis

required_inputs: adjacent specialist map
authority boundary and handoff prerequisites
required evidence package for the adjacent owner
refusal_triggers: request belongs to patient access, care coordination, healthcare IT, revenue cycle, utilization management, clinical leadership, privacy, staffing office, emergency management, payer operations, or compliance ownership
adjacent owner cannot be uniquely identified

required_inputs: accepted operational request
tenant lifecycle-state definitions and closure criteria
current artifacts, owner, due date, escalation SLA, and system links
refusal_triggers: state cannot move forward because prerequisite evidence, owner acceptance, system record, or approval is missing
requested transition would bypass clinical, privacy, payer, staffing, or executive authority

required_inputs: exception type, severity, service impact, owner, due time, escalation rule, and source evidence
capacity, staffing, system, prerequisite, privacy, clinical, and payer boundary signals
tenant prioritization and escalation rules
refusal_triggers: exception requires authority outside operations lane
service impact cannot be assessed without missing records or owner confirmation
policy conflict or patient-safety indicator prevents administrative resolution

required_inputs: resolved-state evidence
owner acceptance, residual blocker, audit log, and system-of-record links
reopen criteria and downstream handoff confirmation
refusal_triggers: closure is requested without owner acceptance or auditable record links
residual clinical, privacy, payer, staffing, or system risk remains unresolved

required_inputs: request content and patient-safety indicators
clinical protocol, treatment, discharge, triage, licensure, and care-team authority map
administrative facts that can be summarized safely
refusal_triggers: request requires diagnosis, treatment planning, triage, clinical protocol change, discharge readiness, patient-specific clinical priority, or staffing licensure judgment
operational delay includes patient-safety indicators that require licensed review

required_inputs: PHI handling context, role-based access posture, consent/proxy and minimum-necessary rules
patient-safety, capacity, downtime, staffing, and clinical-risk indicators
privacy, clinical, command, or compliance owner map
refusal_triggers: request seeks PHI disclosure, patient-level export, or broad distribution without documented authorization
capacity, downtime, staffing, or clinical signal may affect patient safety and requires accountable clinical or command owner
